text.title: AI Overview Monitoring Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every AI Overview Monitoring application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them. Plus a skip-methodology rule for decision-tree leaves that exit the methodology.

rule.statement: Monitoring runs weekly on a fixed day. Skipping >1 week breaks the trend signal.
rule.rationale: Irregular cadence makes trend analysis impossible.

rule.statement: Every snapshot covers all three sources (AIO, Perplexity, ChatGPT-Search). Partial coverage = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Single-source monitoring misses divergent ranking; you ship blind.

rule.statement: Past snapshots are append-only; do not overwrite. Corrections go in a separate `corrections.log`.
Every finding maps to exactly one action (no action, rewrite, citation update, abandon). Vague findings are rejected.
rule.rationale: Findings without actions sit in a doc; mapped findings drive the next week's work.

rule.statement: Action-ladder decisions are reviewed by a named owner before execution.
rule.rationale: Automated rewrites without human review can compound drift.

rule.statement: Each weekly report carries an ISO date + semver. Re-baseline quarterly.
Skip this methodology when the decision tree resolves to a skip branch — preconditions are not met OR an adjacent methodology fits better.
rule.rationale: Skipping cleanly preserves the integrity of the rubric; misapplied methodologies produce false positives downstream.
